skill.name: orchestrator-agent
skill.description: Coordinates Unite-Hub workflows by validating readiness, routing tasks to specialist agents, tracking memory, handling errors, and producing operational reports.
skill.summary: Coordinates Unite-Hub agent workflows with validation, routing, memory, error handling, and reporting.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell backtick and blocker patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences and descriptive workflow text. The remaining real concern is that the skill instructs an assistant to run local npm agent commands and perform approval or audit mutations in Unite-Hub workflows, so publication should include a clear operational warning.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Can Trigger Local Agent Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the orchestrator to call npm run email-agent and npm run content-agent. This is legitimate for a workflow coordinator, but it can execute local project scripts and should run only in a trusted Unite-Hub repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is explicit and located in workflow instructions. The risk is contextual because the file is guidance, not executable code, and the command targets expected project agents.

medium_findings.title: Automated Business Data Mutations Require Review
medium_findings.description: The skill describes approving content with a system user, updating contact follow-up fields, and posting audit events. These operations are expected for Unite-Hub, but mistakes could alter customer or campaign records.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly describe mutations and audit writes. I did not find evidence of malicious intent, but the workflow can affect production business data if used without safeguards.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer classified Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are documentation blocks, not executable Ruby, shell, or JavaScript code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fences around examples and reports. No evidence found that these fences execute code by themselves.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches do not identify cryptographic code. Line 3 is front matter text, and line 131 contains DESC as a sort direction, not the DES algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text is visible in the skill documentation and is not a cryptographic API or algorithm selection. No evidence found of encryption or hashing behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance matches are ordinary workflow health-check descriptions and error categories. They do not instruct scanning hosts, enumerating networks, or collecting system secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe validating users, contacts, a network blip, and invalid contact data. No evidence found of network probing or unauthorized system discovery.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Orchestration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the assistant to execute project npm scripts for email and content agents. This should be limited to trusted repositories and reviewed package scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command invocations are explicitly documented. The danger depends on the implementation of the referenced npm scripts, which is outside this skill file.

content.value_statement: Multi-agent CRM workflows can stall when agents run without validation or shared state. This skill coordinates validation, routing, memory, error handling, and reporting for Unite-Hub automations.
actual_capabilities: Routes high-level requests through readiness validation before specialist agents run.
Coordinates email-agent and content-agent pipeline steps with progress reports.
Defines approval and scheduling flow for generated content drafts.
Specifies health checks for data integrity, audit logs, database status, and agent performance.
Describes persistent memory keys for workflow state and run history.
Provides report templates for pipeline completion and system health.
limitations: Designed for Unite-Hub data models, agent names, and workflow conventions.
Does not include executable scripts; it depends on existing npm agent commands.
Database actions are described as procedures, not implemented in this skill file.
Claude API integration is mentioned as future work, not a complete integration.

use_cases.title: Run Sales Follow-Up Pipelines
use_cases.description: Coordinate email processing, lead scoring, content generation, and completion reporting for warm prospects.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Blocked Automations
use_cases.description: Validate build status, type safety, dependencies, and agent errors before assigning work to specialists.

use_cases.title: Review System Health
use_cases.description: Summarize data integrity, audit log errors, database health, and recent agent performance in one report.

prompt_templates.title: Check Workflow Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the orchestrator-agent skill to check pending content status and summarize what needs review next.

prompt_templates.title: Run Email To Content Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the orchestrator-agent skill to process new emails, identify warm leads, generate follow-up content, and report the results.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose A Blocked Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the orchestrator-agent skill to validate readiness, find the blocker, classify the error level, and recommend a recovery path.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Recovery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the orchestrator-agent skill to design a recovery plan for failing email and content agents, including memory updates and audit reporting.

output_examples.input: Run full workflow: process emails and generate content.
output_examples.output: A concise pipeline report with email counts, updated contacts, generated drafts, high-priority leads, errors, and recommended next actions.
A health summary covering data integrity, recent errors, database consistency, agent performance, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: Approve all content for two named contacts.
output_examples.output: A validation summary, approval status for each draft, contact updates, audit trail confirmation, and any blocked items.

best_practices: Run readiness validation before starting email, content, or approval workflows.
Review package scripts and database permissions before allowing command execution.
Use audit logs and workflow memory to preserve traceability across runs.
anti_patterns: Do not run the orchestrator in an unknown repository without reviewing npm scripts.
Do not approve or schedule generated content without human policy checks.
Do not treat health reports as proof that external services are available.

faq.question: What does this skill coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates Unite-Hub validation, email processing, content generation, approvals, health checks, memory updates, and reporting.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill file provides workflow instructions and examples. It expects existing Unite-Hub agents and npm scripts.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The file is documentation, but it instructs assistants to run local commands and perform business data mutations.

faq.question: Can it work outside Unite-Hub?
faq.answer: Only with adaptation. The workflows reference Unite-Hub tables, memory keys, agent names, and audit conventions.

faq.question: Does it handle failed workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It defines recoverable, significant, and critical error levels with logging, retry, escalation, and halt behavior.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
